messages.content:
Statistical inference is


about hypothesis testing and interpreting p-value


numerical and graphical summary of data in a sample


the process of drawing conclusions carefully based on data of the entire population


drawing conclusions on the population parameters of interest based on sampled data
drawing conclusions on the population parameters of interest based on sampled data

Sampling error in statistical inference is best described as


the unavoidable difference between sample estimate and population parameter because the sample does not include every element in the population.


untruthful answers from survey respondents.


the crafting of survey questions in such a biased way that a desired response will be elicited more easily from respondents.


unintentional mistakes a researcher makes when collecting data.
the unavoidable difference between sample estimate and population parameter because the sample does not include every element in the population.

A health product company's customer wellbeing officer is planning a survey of the stress from heavy workload among the consumers of the company's products. She is thinking of inviting customers via the company's official Facebook page to complete an online anonymous questionnaire. What is(are) the potential sampling bias(es) of this sampling strategy?


Self-selection bias


Neither under-coverage bias nor self-selection bias


Under-coverage bias


Both under-coverage bias and self-selection bias
Both under-coverage bias and self-selection bias

A clinic manager wants to determine the proportion of patients who are referred by their primary care physician to another physician within the group versus a physician outside the group. Every 100th patient record is selected and reviewed for a letter from a physician other than the primary care physician. This sampling method is best described as


Purposive sampling


Systematic sampling


Stratified sampling


Simple random sampling

Which of the following non-random sampling methods is similar to random stratified sampling?


Quota sampling


Purposive sampling


Snowball sampling


Convenience sampling

Which statemen below is the best description of the sampling distribution of a sample statistic?


The distribution of the sample statistic across all samples that are of different sizes and randomly selected from the population.


The distribution of the sample statistic across all possible samples that are of the same size and randomly selected from the population.


The distribution of the variable of interest in the population.


The distribution of the variable of interest in a sample.
The distribution of the sample statistic across all possible samples that are of the same size and randomly selected from the population.

Given random sampling, which of the following statement on central limit theorem is true?


The standard error of the mean shrinks when sample size increases.


All the three statements are parts of the central limit theorem.


Regardless of the population's distribution shape, the sampling distribution of sample mean approaches a normal distribution the sample size increases.


Sampling distribution of sample mean approaches the population mean as sample gets larger.
All the three statements are parts of the central limit theorem.

Which of the following conceptions of the p-value is correct?


The p-value is the probability that the null hypothesis is true.


The p-value indicates the practical importance of research finding.


The p-value is the probability that the alternative hypothesis is false.


The p-value partially depends on sample size and should not be used as the only metric to judge the importance of evidence in the sample.
The p-value partially depends on sample size and should not be used as the only metric to judge the importance of evidence in the sample.

The clean air legislation in Australia requires that vehicle exhaust emissions not exceed certain limits for various types of pollutants. In Queensland, the Department of Environment has recently received complaints that some car repair workshops certified vehicles that do not meet the emission standard. The regulator decides to check a random sample of cars that a suspect repair shop has certified as okay. They will revoke the shop's license if they find significant evidence that the shop is issuing certificates for cars that do not meet the standard. Which type of error in hypothesis testing might the environmentalists consider more serious?


Type II error: revoke the license when a shop is meeting standards.


Type I error: the shop is certified as meeting standards when it is not.


Type II error: the shop is certified as meeting standards when it is not.


Type I error: revoke the license when a shop is meeting standards.
Type II error: revoke the license when a shop is meeting standards.
